framework.name: ISO/IEC 42005 - AI System Impact Assessment
framework.description: ISO/IEC 42005 provides guidance for assessing the potential impacts of AI systems on individuals, groups, communities, society, and the environment throughout the AI system lifecycle. It supports organizations in identifying, analyzing, evaluating, treating, and monitoring impacts, aligning with ISO 31000 risk management and ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS requirements.
framework.verification_notes: Rebuilt from actual ISO/IEC 42005:2025 standard (May 2025, First Edition). Real structure: Clause 5 (process, 5.2-5.12) and Clause 6 (documentation, 6.2-6.9). Impact dimensions moved to Annex C (Harms and benefits taxonomy, informative). Previous fabricated codes (IAP.x, ID.x, SE.x, LC.x) replaced with actual clause references. 50 concepts total.

concepts.name_en: Developing and Implementing an AI System Impact Assessment Process
concepts.name_nb: Utvikle og implementere en prosess for KI-systemkonsekvensvurdering
concepts.definition_en: Clause 5 provides guidance on developing and implementing an organisational process for AI system impact assessment (AIIA), covering process documentation, integration with other management processes, timing, scope, responsibilities, thresholds, performing and analysing the assessment, recording, approval, and ongoing monitoring.
concepts.definition_nb: Klausul 5 gir veiledning om utvikling og implementering av en organisatorisk prosess for KI-systemkonsekvensvurdering, inkludert prosessdokumentasjon, integrering med andre styringsprosesser, timing, omfang, ansvar, terskler, gjennomføring og analyse.

concepts.definition_en: The organisation shall document its AI system impact assessment process, including how it is initiated, carried out, reviewed, and updated. Documentation supports consistency, reproducibility, and auditability of impact assessments across the organisation.
concepts.definition_nb: Organisasjonen skal dokumentere sin prosess for KI-systemkonsekvensvurdering, inkludert hvordan den igangsettes, gjennomføres, gjennomgås og oppdateres.

concepts.name_en: Integration with Other Organisational Management Processes
concepts.definition_en: The AI system impact assessment process should be integrated with existing organisational processes such as risk management (ISO 31000, ISO 23894), AI management systems (ISO 42001), data protection impact assessment (GDPR Art. 35), and product/service development lifecycles to avoid duplication and ensure coherent governance.
concepts.definition_nb: Prosessen for KI-systemkonsekvensvurdering bør integreres med eksisterende organisatoriske prosesser som risikostyring, KI-styringssystemer og personvernkonsekvensvurderinger.

concepts.name_en: Timing of AI System Impact Assessment
concepts.definition_en: Guidance on when to initiate an AI system impact assessment throughout the AI system lifecycle — including during design, development, pre-deployment, deployment, and post-deployment phases. Reassessment is required when the AI system, its use context, or applicable requirements change materially.
concepts.definition_nb: Veiledning om når man skal starte en KI-systemkonsekvensvurdering gjennom hele KI-systemets livssyklus, inkludert under design, utvikling, pre-distribusjon, distribusjon og post-distribusjonsfaser.

concepts.name_en: Scope of the AI System Impact Assessment
concepts.definition_en: Defining what is included in an individual impact assessment: the AI system boundary, the intended and foreseeable uses, the affected populations and stakeholders, and the geographic/temporal scope. Clear scope prevents gaps and avoids unnecessary duplication with other assessments.
concepts.definition_nb: Definering av hva som inngår i en individuell konsekvensvurdering: KI-systemets grenser, tiltenkte og forutsigbare bruksområder, berørte befolkninger og interessenter, og geografisk/tidsmessig omfang.

concepts.definition_en: Identifying and assigning roles and responsibilities for conducting, reviewing, approving, and maintaining AI system impact assessments. Includes accountability for the assessment owner, subject matter experts (ethics, legal, technical, domain), impacted stakeholder representatives, and senior management approval.
concepts.definition_nb: Identifisering og tildeling av roller og ansvar for gjennomføring, gjennomgang, godkjenning og vedlikehold av KI-systemkonsekvensvurderinger.

concepts.name_en: Establishing Thresholds for Sensitive Uses, Restricted Uses and Impact Scales
concepts.name_nb: Etablere terskler for sensitiv bruk, begrenset bruk og konsekvensomfang
concepts.definition_en: Defining criteria to categorise AI system uses as sensitive (requiring enhanced assessment), restricted (requiring special justification), or prohibited. Establishes scales for measuring the magnitude, likelihood, and reversibility of impacts, enabling consistent prioritisation and decision-making across assessments.
concepts.definition_nb: Definering av kriterier for å kategorisere KI-systembruk som sensitiv, begrenset eller forbudt, og etablering av skalaer for å måle omfang, sannsynlighet og reversibilitet av konsekvenser.

concepts.name_en: Performing the AI System Impact Assessment
concepts.definition_en: Guidance on conducting the assessment: identifying affected interested parties, characterising the AI system and its deployment context, systematically identifying potential harms and benefits across all relevant impact dimensions (see Annex C), assessing their likelihood and magnitude, and engaging stakeholders throughout.
concepts.definition_nb: Veiledning om gjennomføring av vurderingen: identifisere berørte interessenter, karakterisere KI-systemet og dets distribusjonskontekst, systematisk identifisere potensielle skader og fordeler.

concepts.name_en: Analysing the Results of the AI System Impact Assessment
concepts.definition_en: Interpreting assessment findings to determine the overall impact profile of the AI system: comparing identified harms and benefits against established thresholds, determining whether sensitive or restricted use criteria are triggered, and identifying which harms require treatment measures.
concepts.definition_nb: Tolking av vurderingsfunn for å bestemme det samlede konsekvensprofilen til KI-systemet: sammenligne identifiserte skader og fordeler mot etablerte terskler.

concepts.definition_en: Requirements for creating, maintaining, and sharing AI system impact assessment records. Includes the format and content of the AIIA document (detailed in Clause 6), internal reporting to decision-makers, and where required, external disclosure to regulators, affected parties, or the public.
concepts.definition_nb: Krav til opprettelse, vedlikehold og deling av poster for KI-systemkonsekvensvurdering, inkludert format og innhold i AIIA-dokumentet (detaljert i klausul 6).

concepts.definition_en: Guidance on establishing a formal approval step before deploying or significantly modifying an AI system. Approval confirms that identified harms have been adequately assessed, treatment measures are in place, and accountable parties have accepted residual risks. Approval records form part of the audit trail.
concepts.definition_nb: Veiledning om etablering av et formelt godkjenningstrinn før distribusjon eller vesentlig modifisering av et KI-system. Godkjenning bekrefter at identifiserte skader er tilstrekkelig vurdert.

concepts.definition_en: Ongoing monitoring of deployed AI systems to detect whether actual impacts differ from assessed impacts, and triggering reassessment when material changes occur (new use cases, changed deployment context, emerging harms, regulatory updates). Connects impact assessment to the AI system lifecycle management process.
concepts.definition_nb: Løpende overvåking av distribuerte KI-systemer for å oppdage om faktiske konsekvenser avviker fra vurderte konsekvenser, og utløse ny vurdering ved vesentlige endringer.

concepts.name_en: Documenting the AI System Impact Assessment
concepts.definition_en: Clause 6 specifies what an AI system impact assessment document shall contain: scope, AI system information (description, capabilities, purpose, intended/unintended uses), data information, algorithm/model information, deployment environment, relevant interested parties, identified impacts, and measures to address harms.
concepts.definition_nb: Klausul 6 spesifiserer hva et KI-systemkonsekvensvurderingsdokument skal inneholde: omfang, KI-systeminformasjon, datainformasjon, algoritme-/modellinformasjon, distribusjonsmiljø, berørte interessenter, identifiserte konsekvenser og tiltak.

concepts.definition_en: Documentation of the AI system being assessed: its description, functionalities and capabilities, purpose, intended uses, and unintended uses that are reasonably foreseeable. This section establishes the factual baseline against which impacts are assessed.
concepts.definition_nb: Dokumentasjon av KI-systemet som vurderes: beskrivelse, funksjoner og kapasiteter, formål, tiltenkte bruksområder og rimelig forutsigbare utilsiktede bruksområder.

concepts.definition_en: Documentation of data used in the AI system: data sources, collection methods, processing applied, and quality characteristics relevant to impact. Data biases, gaps, or quality issues that could amplify harms (e.g. discriminatory outcomes) shall be documented.
concepts.definition_nb: Dokumentasjon av data brukt i KI-systemet: datakilder, innsamlingsmetoder, behandling og kvalitetsegenskaper relevante for konsekvenser. Databias og kvalitetsproblemer skal dokumenteres.

concepts.definition_en: Documentation of algorithms and models used in the AI system, including information on algorithms and models used by the organisation, how they were developed, and relevant properties (e.g. explainability, fairness characteristics, known limitations) that affect the impact assessment.
concepts.definition_nb: Dokumentasjon av algoritmer og modeller brukt i KI-systemet, inkludert informasjon om algoritmer og modeller brukt av organisasjonen, hvordan de ble utviklet og relevante egenskaper.

concepts.definition_en: Documentation of the environment in which the AI system operates: geographical area and languages, deployment context complexity and constraints (technical, organisational, regulatory, social). The deployment environment shapes which impacts are likely and who is affected.
concepts.definition_nb: Dokumentasjon av miljøet der KI-systemet opererer: geografisk område og språk, kompleksitet og begrensninger i distribusjonskonteksten (tekniske, organisatoriske, regulatoriske, sosiale).

concepts.definition_en: Identification and documentation of parties affected by or with an interest in the AI system: directly affected parties (individuals whose rights or interests are directly impacted by the AI system's outputs or decisions) and other relevant interested parties (regulators, civil society, supply chain actors). Includes specific attention to vulnerable and marginalised groups.
concepts.definition_nb: Identifisering og dokumentasjon av parter berørt av eller med interesse i KI-systemet: direkte berørte parter og andre relevante interessenter. Inkluderer særlig oppmerksomhet på sårbare og marginaliserte grupper.

concepts.definition_en: Documentation of the identified benefits and harms of the AI system, including impacts from AI system failures and reasonably foreseeable misuse. Each impact is characterised by type, affected parties, likelihood, magnitude, and reversibility. This section references the harms and benefits taxonomy in Annex C.
concepts.definition_nb: Dokumentasjon av de identifiserte fordelene og skadene til KI-systemet, inkludert konsekvenser av KI-systemfeil og rimelig forutsigbar misbruk.

concepts.name_en: Measures to Address Harms and Benefits
concepts.name_nb: Tiltak for å håndtere skader og fordeler
concepts.definition_en: Documentation of measures taken or planned to mitigate identified harms and enhance benefits: technical controls (fairness constraints, safety measures, access controls), procedural controls (human oversight, appeals mechanisms), and monitoring measures. Includes residual risk acceptance and responsible parties.
concepts.definition_nb: Dokumentasjon av tiltak for å redusere identifiserte skader og forsterke fordeler: tekniske kontroller, prosedyremessige kontroller og overvåkingstiltak.

concepts.name_nb: Taksonomi for skader og fordeler (vedlegg C)
concepts.definition_en: Informative Annex C provides a taxonomy of harms and benefits for use when performing AI system impact assessments. It is structured around six impact dimensions: human rights and fundamental freedoms, health and safety, societal wellbeing, economic impacts, environmental impacts, and governance and accountability.
concepts.definition_nb: Informativt vedlegg C gir en taksonomi over skader og fordeler for bruk ved gjennomføring av KI-systemkonsekvensvurderinger, strukturert rundt seks konsekvensdimensjoner.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on legally recognised human rights and fundamental freedoms, including privacy, non-discrimination, freedom of expression, due process, and human dignity. AI systems can threaten these rights through surveillance, profiling, automated decision-making, or censorship.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for juridisk anerkjente menneskerettigheter og grunnleggende friheter, inkludert personvern, ikke-diskriminering, ytringsfrihet, rettssikkerhet og menneskelig verdighet.

concepts.definition_en: The right of individuals to control information about themselves. AI systems can undermine privacy through data collection, inference, tracking, profiling, or enabling unauthorised disclosure.
concepts.definition_nb: Retten for enkeltpersoner til å kontrollere informasjon om seg selv.

concepts.definition_en: Protection against unfair treatment based on protected characteristics. AI systems can perpetuate or amplify discrimination through biased training data, proxy variables, or differential performance across demographic groups.
concepts.definition_nb: Beskyttelse mot urettferdig behandling basert på beskyttede egenskaper.

concepts.definition_en: The right to hold and express opinions without interference. AI systems used for content moderation, automated censorship, disinformation generation, or opinion manipulation may restrict or distort free expression.
concepts.definition_nb: Retten til å ha og uttrykke meninger uten innblanding.

concepts.definition_en: The right to fair treatment through established procedures, including the right to explanation, contestation, and appeal of automated decisions. AI-driven decisions without transparency or recourse mechanisms undermine due process.
concepts.definition_nb: Retten til rettferdig behandling gjennom etablerte prosedyrer, inkludert retten til forklaring og klage på automatiserte beslutninger.

concepts.definition_en: The inherent worth of every person that must not be violated. AI systems can threaten human dignity through dehumanising treatment, exploitation, psychological manipulation, or treating persons merely as data points.
concepts.definition_nb: Den iboende verdien til ethvert menneske som ikke må krenkes.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on physical health, mental health, and safety of individuals. AI systems may introduce safety risks through failures in safety-critical applications, or health harms through mental health effects of algorithmic systems.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for fysisk helse, mental helse og sikkerhet for enkeltpersoner.

concepts.definition_en: Risks of physical harm or injury from AI system failures, particularly in safety-critical domains such as autonomous vehicles, medical devices, industrial robotics, or infrastructure control systems.
concepts.definition_nb: Risiko for fysisk skade fra KI-systemfeil, særlig i sikkerhetskritiske domener.

concepts.definition_en: Psychological harms caused by AI systems, including anxiety or distress from algorithmic decisions, harmful content recommendation, social media addiction mechanisms, or AI-enabled harassment.
concepts.definition_nb: Psykologiske skader forårsaket av KI-systemer, inkludert angst fra algoritmiske beslutninger og skadelig innholdsanbefaling.

concepts.definition_en: Safety and health impacts on workers from AI systems used in work environments, including workplace monitoring, algorithmic management, and AI-driven workload or pace control that may cause physical or psychological occupational hazards.
concepts.definition_nb: Sikkerhets- og helsemessige konsekvenser for arbeidstakere fra KI-systemer brukt i arbeidssammenhenger.

concepts.definition_en: Broader social impacts beyond individual harms: effects on social cohesion, democratic processes, public trust in institutions, cultural practices, and equitable access to public and private services.
concepts.definition_nb: Bredere sosiale konsekvenser utover individuelle skader: effekter på sosial samhørighet, demokratiske prosesser, offentlig tillit og rettferdig tilgang til tjenester.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on the bonds and solidarity within communities. AI systems used for personalisation or recommendation may create filter bubbles, echo chambers, and polarisation that fragment social cohesion.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for båndene og solidariteten innen samfunn.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on elections, political participation, and democratic institutions. AI systems capable of generating disinformation, micro-targeting voters, or manipulating public opinion pose risks to democratic integrity.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for valg, politisk deltakelse og demokratiske institusjoner.

concepts.definition_en: Trust in institutions, services, and public systems. Opaque, erroneous, or unfair AI-driven decisions — particularly in public services — can erode trust in government and institutions.
concepts.definition_nb: Tillit til institusjoner, tjenester og offentlige systemer.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on cultural diversity, languages, heritage, and the creative economy. AI systems trained on dominant cultural data may marginalise minority languages and cultures; generative AI may displace creative professions.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for kulturelt mangfold, språk, kulturarv og den kreative økonomien.

concepts.definition_en: Equitable access to essential services mediated by AI systems (healthcare, financial services, housing, employment, education). Biased or opaque AI-driven gatekeeping can perpetuate or worsen existing inequalities in access.
concepts.definition_nb: Rettferdig tilgang til essensielle tjenester formidlet av KI-systemer (helsevesen, finansielle tjenester, bolig, arbeid, utdanning).

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on labour markets, economic inequality, market structure, and innovation capacity. AI automation and optimisation create both economic benefits and harms that vary across populations and industries.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for arbeidsmarkeder, økonomisk ulikhet, markedsstruktur og innovasjonskapasitet.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on jobs, working conditions, and labour markets from AI-driven automation. Includes both displacement of roles and creation of new roles, with distributional effects that may worsen inequalities.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for arbeidsplasser, arbeidsforhold og arbeidsmarkeder fra KI-drevet automatisering.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on the distribution of wealth and economic opportunity. AI systems may concentrate economic gains among those with access to AI capabilities while widening inequality for others.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for fordelingen av velstand og økonomiske muligheter.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on competitive market structures. AI capabilities can create or reinforce dominant market positions, raise barriers to entry, or enable anti-competitive coordination, affecting consumers and smaller competitors.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for konkurransemessige markedsstrukturer.

concepts.definition_en: Both the enabling of beneficial innovation (new products, services, research acceleration) and the suppression of innovation through lock-in effects, IP concerns, or regulatory risk aversion caused by AI systems.
concepts.definition_nb: Både muliggjøring av gunstig innovasjon og undertrykkelse av innovasjon gjennom innlåsningseffekter eller regulatorisk risikoaversjon.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on the natural environment from AI systems throughout their lifecycle, including energy consumption during training and inference, resource use, carbon emissions, electronic waste, and broader ecological effects.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for det naturlige miljøet fra KI-systemer gjennom hele livssyklusen, inkludert energiforbruk, ressursbruk, karbonutslipp og elektronisk avfall.

concepts.definition_en: The energy demands of AI system training, inference, and infrastructure. Large language models and compute-intensive AI require significant energy, contributing to carbon emissions if sourced from fossil fuels.
concepts.definition_nb: Energibehovet til KI-systemtrening, inferens og infrastruktur.

concepts.definition_en: Consumption of physical resources for AI hardware: rare earth minerals, water for data centre cooling, and land use for infrastructure. Resource extraction for AI chips has significant environmental and geopolitical implications.
concepts.definition_nb: Forbruk av fysiske ressurser for KI-maskinvare: sjeldne jordartsmetaller, vann til kjøling og landbruk til infrastruktur.

concepts.definition_en: Greenhouse gas emissions from AI system operations, including Scope 1 (direct), Scope 2 (energy), and Scope 3 (supply chain) emissions. Relevant to organisational climate commitments and regulatory carbon reporting.
concepts.definition_nb: Klimagassutslipp fra KI-systemoperasjoner, inkludert direkte, energi- og forsyningskjedeutslipp.

concepts.definition_en: Electronic waste generated by the rapid obsolescence of AI hardware (GPUs, TPUs, specialised chips). Improper disposal of e-waste releases toxic materials and represents a loss of recoverable resources.
concepts.definition_nb: Elektronisk avfall generert av rask foreldelse av KI-maskinvare.

concepts.definition_en: Broader ecological impacts from AI deployment: habitat disruption from data centre construction, water use affecting local ecosystems, or AI-optimised resource extraction that accelerates environmental degradation.
concepts.definition_nb: Bredere økologiske konsekvenser fra KI-distribusjon: habitatforstyrrelse, vannbruk og KI-optimalisert ressursutvinning.

concepts.definition_en: Impacts on the ability to govern AI systems and hold actors accountable: transparency of AI operations, oversight mechanisms, redress for harm, and concentration of AI-related power that may reduce societal ability to govern the technology.
concepts.definition_nb: Konsekvenser for evnen til å styre KI-systemer og holde aktører ansvarlige: transparens, tilsynsmekanismer, oppreisning for skade og maktkonsentrasjon.

concepts.definition_en: The degree to which AI system operations, decision logic, and impacts are understandable and disclosed to affected parties and the public. Lack of transparency undermines accountability and informed consent.
concepts.definition_nb: I hvilken grad KI-systemoperasjoner, beslutningslogikk og konsekvenser er forståelige og avslørt for berørte parter og offentligheten.

concepts.definition_en: The capacity of humans, organisations, and regulators to monitor, audit, and control AI systems. AI systems that resist oversight — through complexity, opacity, or speed — reduce the effectiveness of governance mechanisms.
concepts.definition_nb: Kapasiteten til mennesker, organisasjoner og regulatorer til å overvåke, revidere og kontrollere KI-systemer.

concepts.definition_en: Mechanisms for individuals harmed by AI systems to seek correction, explanation, or remedy. Effective redress requires identifying the responsible party, understanding what decision was made and why, and having access to an appeals process.
concepts.definition_nb: Mekanismer for enkeltpersoner skadet av KI-systemer til å søke korrigering, forklaring eller oppreisning.

concepts.definition_en: Risks arising from concentration of AI capabilities, data, or infrastructure in a small number of organisations or states, reducing pluralism, competitive balance, and democratic oversight of AI development and deployment.
concepts.definition_nb: Risikoer fra konsentrasjon av KI-kapasiteter, data eller infrastruktur i et lite antall organisasjoner eller stater.
